description: Lock distillation_advanced to the ninth-century medieval alembic boundary: Arabic-language alchemical and perfume-making apparatus with controlled heat, evidenced glass and pottery components, no generic bronze-working prerequisite, and no reverse-causal dependency from the c. 270 basic_chemistry node.

checks.reason: The century anchor follows al-Kindi's ninth-century apparatus and procedure rather than Zosimos's Late Antique activity.
Jabirian and al-Razi alchemical practice shares the documented apparatus and heating methods.
Alchemy enables the experimental tradition but transmutation theory is not required for perfume distillation.
The documented unit includes a glass receiver.
Glass is an evidenced component route, not the sole possible apparatus material.
The method requires sustained water-bath or charcoal-stove heating.
Distillation ceases when the bath cools, making controlled heat part of the operating mechanism.
The documented moist-heating apparatus uses an earthenware or pottery bath vessel.
Pottery enables the cited bath vessel but is not universal to every apparatus component.
The scoped ninth-century apparatus is documented without bronze, and no source ties copper-tin alloy craft to this milestone.
The c. 270 node cannot depend on the separately scoped ninth-century medieval alembic milestone.
